Costco is taking a methodical, operations-first approach to AI implementation, prioritizing practical applications that reinforce its low-cost membership model over flashy customer-facing technologies[1][2]. CEO Ron Vachris describes the retailer as approaching AI 'in a very Costco way, practical, member-focused and grounded in tangible business value'[2]. The company has been quietly integrating AI across its operations since at least 2018, when it launched a machine learning pilot for bakery demand forecasting that ultimately saved nearly $100 million and was adopted company-wide[3]. This measured approach to AI adoption has accelerated significantly in 2025, with major initiatives including scan-and-go checkout technology showing 20% speed improvements[4], enhanced search functionality using Google's Vertex AI[5], and the rollout of AI-powered retail media capabilities through partnerships with GrowthLoop[6].
